mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.05) or negative mean returns during periods of falling FEDFUNDS over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 5.0 percent signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances when UNRATE crosses above 5.0%, or Rank IC falls below 0.02 over any 24-month period.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer and business demand expansion, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns with an out-of-sample accuracy below 52%, or interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the industrial production index reflects weakening manufacturing output that reduces corporate earnings forecasts, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over one month.
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO growth correlates with positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in over 60% of observations, or the mechanism yields a negative Sharpe ratio in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index growth exceeding 2.5 percent annually forces expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, which compresses equity valuations and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 5-day returns remain positive in more than 58% of periods when CPIAUCSL exceeds the 2.5% threshold, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls combined with falling unemployment rates confirm robust labor market tightening that supports corporate revenue growth, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over one month.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE fails to produce positive 20-day returns with statistical significance, or the interaction effect reverses direction in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly gross domestic product growth above trend levels confirms broad economic expansion that lifts corporate profit margins, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Positive GDPC1 growth rates correspond to negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of cases, or the hypothesis yields a negative information coefficient over any 36-month rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in industrial production exceeding 1 percent signals acute supply-side disruption that triggers risk-off positioning, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in over 60% of instances following a >1% monthly drop in INDPRO, or the trigger fails to maintain a positive predictive accuracy above 53% in walk-forward validation.
